items.title: Malaysia's prosperity must create opportunities, not just growth
items.summary: This is the central purpose of Ekonomi Madani, he said in Khazanah Nasional Bhd's The Khazanah Report 2025 released tonight. Anwar said these frameworks aim to move Malaysia towards higher-value economic activities and strengthen its position in emerging industries. Anwar said Khazanah's mandate extends beyond short-term financial returns.

items.title: EC to decide Negri Sembilan polls dates on June 12
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Election Commission (EC) will hold a special meeting on June 12 to discuss the 16th Negri Sembilan state election. EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the meeting, which coincides with the 16th Johor state election special meeting, will be held following the dissolution of the 15th Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly last Friday. He said the meeting would be chaired by EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun who would discuss important dates for the Negri Sembilan state election.

items.title: Charity group pledges yearly support for public healthcare, donates RM1m in equipment
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 8 — Penang-based non-governmental organisation One Hope Charity has committed to allocating an annual budget from funds it raises to support government hospitals and clinics. He said hospitals equipped with adequate medical facilities would enable healthcare workers to perform their duties more efficiently and effectively. In previous years, the organisation also donated equipment to government hospitals in Sabah and Sarawak.

items.title: AirBorneo says flight disruptions to continue as concurrent aircraft maintenance hits Sabah, Sarawak services
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — AirBorneo has confirmed ongoing flight disruptions across Sabah and Sarawak, with delays and cancellations expected to continue over the coming days due to concurrent aircraft maintenance and technical rectification works. It said the situation was caused by a combination of unscheduled technical rectification on several aircraft, scheduled maintenance already underway, and operational and crew duty requirements. AirBorneo said aircraft requiring technical attention had been withdrawn from service as a precaution until engineers were fully satisfied they were fit to fly.

items.title: Gold hits more than two-month low as strong US jobs data boosts rate-hike bets
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold prices hit a more than two-month low on Monday after last week's strong US jobs data boosted expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike, and as Israel and Iran traded strikes, pushing oil prices higher and fuelling inflation concerns. US gold futures for August delivery were down 0.6 per cent at US$4,340.90. Spot silver was down 0.3 per cent at US$67.6 per ounce, platinum lost 0.2 per cent to US$1,772.72, and palladium fell 0.2 per cent to US$1,223.50.

items.title: Anwar arrives in Tokyo for three-day Japan visit, Nikkei Forum on agenda
items.summary: TOKYO, June 8 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim arrived here tonight for a three-day official visit to Japan, aimed at further strengthening bilateral ties and participating in the 31st Nikkei Forum. The aircraft carrying the Prime Minister touched down at Haneda International Airport at 8.20pm local time (7.20pm Malaysia time). The visit is Anwar’s first official trip to Japan since assuming office as Malaysia’s 10th Prime Minister in November 2022, although he previously made two working visits in 2023 and 2024.

items.title: Keyfield buys anchor handling tug supply vessel for RM29.6mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Offshore vessel operator Keyfield International Bhd will buy a 2014-built anchor handling tug supply (AHTS) vessel for US$7.35 million (RM29.6 million). In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said its unit Keyfield Resolute Sdn Bhd had signed a memorandum of agreement to but the vessel, which will be renamed Keyfield Joyful. It will immediately expand the company's AHTS capacity and enable Keyfield to capture near-term chartering opportunities.

items.title: #NSTviral: Orange Mitsubishi sparks alarm after reckless driving in Seberang Jaya [WATCH]
items.summary: SEBERANG JAYA: An orange Mitsubishi four-wheel drive has sparked public concern after it was allegedly driven recklessly along Jalan Seberang Jaya here, with videos of the incident going viral today. Videos circulating on social media showed the vehicle moving erratically and repeatedly reversing in the middle of the road. Other videos showed the public successfully stopping the four-wheel drive and instructing the driver to get out of the vehicle.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia issues UMA query to Tanco following share price decline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tanco Holdings Bhd has received an unusual market activity (UMA) query from Bursa Malaysia Securities Bhd following a sharp decline in its share price on Monday. It also asked whether Tanco was aware of any rumour or report concerning its business and affairs that may account for the trading activity. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Lorry slams into Kulai toll office after suspected brake failure
items.summary: KULAI: A lorry crashed into the Kulai Toll Plaza office, believed to have been caused by brake failure. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Tan Seng Lee said police received information on the crash at 4pm from Expressway Mobile Patrol Vehicle (EMPV) personnel. He said preliminary findings showed the 29-year-old lorry driver lost control after reportedly experiencing brake problems before veering to the left side of the expressway and crashing into the toll administration office.

items.title: MACC records 1,095 charges, 563 convictions since 2024; new chief pushes tech-driven crackdown, warns: ‘No more old methods’
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has recorded 1,095 charges and secured 563 convictions from 2,633 investigation papers opened since 2024, said Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman. Abd Halim said the MACC’s focus goes beyond prosecuting offenders, with equal emphasis placed on dismantling corruption networks through aggressive asset recovery measures. “During the period, the MACC seized assets worth RM16.49 million, froze assets amounting to RM425,464.60 and secured forfeitures totalling RM3.42 million.

items.title: Cypark to raise RM52.66mil via private placement for project funding, working capital
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cypark Resources Bhd has proposed a private placement of up to 10 per cent of its issued share capital involving the issuance of up to 82.28 million new shares to third-party investors, which is expected to raise approximately RM52.66 million. Of the total placement proceeds the group plans to utilise RM45 million for the funding of its projects, RM7.07 million for working capital and RM590,000 for the estimated expenses for the private placement. "The group intends to utilise proceeds of up to RM45 million to fund the group's projects.

items.title: Ringgit ends lower against US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended lower against the US dollar today as stronger-than-expected United States (US) labour market data reinforced expectations that the US Federal Reserve would maintain its restrictive monetary policy stance, boosting demand for the greenback, an economist said. At 6pm, the local note depreciated to 4.0715/0760 against the US dollar from last Friday's close of 4.0280/0320. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ringgit eased by 1.04 per cent against the US dollar, in tandem with most Asian currencies, which also traded lower against the greenback.

items.title: Baby abandoned at surau in JB, CCTV leads probe
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A newborn baby boy was found abandoned inside a tote bag at a surau in Taman Daya here yesterday afternoon. Johor Baru (Selatan) police chief Assistant Commissioner Raub Selamat confirmed that a report was lodged after the discovery. He said police are tracking down a man in his 20s who is believed to have abandoned the infant, based on closed-circuit television recordings.

items.title: Interpol-backed crackdown hits Thailand-Malaysia piracy network
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thai authorities, working with international partners, have cracked down on a Thailand-Malaysia online piracy network accused of illegally streaming copyrighted films. Director-General of Thailand's Department of Intellectual Property (DIP) Oramon Sapthaweetham said the operation was carried out under Interpol's Stop Online Piracy (I-SOP) programme with support from the Royal Thai Police, Malaysian police, the Korean National Police Agency (KNPA), and rights holders. Oramon said the operation reflected Thailand's intensified efforts to combat online piracy and protect the film industry, copyright holders, and the creative economy.

items.title: Queen graces Gathering Light Kuala Lumpur conference
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia graced the inaugural Gathering Light Kuala Lumpur conference at KLCC here yesterday. Former president of Yayasan Dakwah Islamiah Malaysia (Yadim), Dr Yusri Mohamad, moderated the session. Following the conference, Raja Zarith Sofiah granted an audience to Habib Ali, Sheikh Dr Ahmad and renowned scholar Sheikh Muhamed Hydara Al-Jilani, chairman of the Qadriyya Association of The Gambia.

items.title: EC: Special meeting on N. Sembilan state election on June 12
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Election Commission (EC) will hold a special meeting on June 12 to discuss the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the meeting, which coincides with the 16th Johor state election special meeting, will be held following the dissolution of the 15th Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly last Friday. He said the meeting would be chaired by EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun who would discuss important dates for the Negeri Sembilan state election.

items.title: MCMC orders immediate improvements to cellular coverage in Kampung Tinagat
items.summary: TAWAU: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has directed telecommunications service providers to take immediate measures to improve the quality of communications coverage in Kampung Tinagat here. In a statement today, MCMC said the directive followed a working visit and assessment of communications coverage and service performance conducted at three identified locations in the village. MCMC said Wireless Broadband Access Service (WBAS) tests found that two of the three locations assessed failed to meet the commission's mandatory standards.

items.title: 'Trust, once broken, is hard to rebuild,' says Pas leader on ties with Bersatu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas' position within Perikatan Nasional (PN) continues to be defined by questions over trust within the coalition. Pas information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari said while he trusted Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin 's intention to maintain good relations with the party, political cooperation must be measured by actions, not words. He said Pas investigations had confirmed the existence of the SDs based on confessions from three former Pas assemblymen , witnessed by party leadership.

items.title: Pekanbaru–Melaka flights to boost health tourism from Indonesia, says exco
items.summary: MELAKA, June 8 — The Pekanbaru-Melaka-Pekanbaru flight route, which will begin operating at Batu Berendam International Airport on Wednesday (June 10), is expected to invigorate the state’s health tourism sector through the arrival of visitors from Indonesia seeking medical treatment in Melaka. State Works, Infrastructure, Public Facilities and Transportation Committee chairman Datuk Hameed Mytheen Kunju Basheer said the flight service, operated by Wings Air, will operate three times a week, namely Wednesdays, Fridays and Sundays. Meanwhile, Hameed said another new route, namely Melaka to Penang, by Batik Air is expected to operate within the next two months.

items.title: Hextar Capital unit wins RM60.4mil dam upgrading job in Perlis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hextar Capital Bhd's subsidiary Legacy Core Sdn Bhd has secured a RM60.4 million subcontract from Jati Segera Sdn Bhd for upgrading works at the Timah Tasoh Dam in Perlis. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, Hextar Capital said Legacy Core has accepted a letter of award (LOA) dated June 8, 2026, from Jati Segera. In a statement, Hextar Capital managing director Johnson Chang said the award recognises Legacy Core's capabilities and growing presence in the construction sector.

items.title: Genting inheritance dispute: Lim Goh Tong’s daughter signed will in hospital, court told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The High Court hearing of the Genting Group family’s will dispute heard today that one of the contested wills was signed at a private hospital while the late Lim Siew Kim, daughter of Lim Goh Tong, was undergoing treatment for ovarian cancer. Low Beng Choo, 68, a lawyer who prepared two wills for the estate of the late Siew Kim, said one of the wills was signed on April 11, 2022. Low: I totally disagree.

items.title: Muhibbah bags RM300mil LNG regasification terminal contract in Lumut
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Muhibbah Engineering (M) Bhd has secured a RM300 million engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) contract from Lumut Maritime Terminal Sdn Bhd (LMTSB). In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the construction company said the contract is for the EPCC of LNG Regasification Terminal 3 (RGT3) marine structure and ancillary works, at Lumut Maritime Terminal 2 (LMT2) in Manjung, Perak. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 8, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower today, weighed down by a sharp sell-off in US technology stocks and heightened geopolitical tensions following fresh missile strikes by Iran. The risk-off sentiment prompted investors across the region to trim exposure to equities, resulting in broad-based weakness across Asian markets. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: High Court to rule Aug 5 on appeal over blogger’s acquittal in YouTube posts involving ex-PM Ismail Sabri, Umno
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8— The High Court here today fixed August 5 to rule on the prosecution’s appeal against the Sessions Court’s acquittal of human rights blogger Jufazli Shi Ahmad. The appeal stems from two charges of making offensive posts against former prime minister Datuk Seri Ismail Sabri Yaakob and Umno via YouTube, and failing to provide a YouTube account password to police officers. Chandran, representing Jufazli, 37.

items.title: BIMB Investment launches Shariah-compliant small-cap fund
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: BIMB Investment Management Bhd, a subsidiary of Bank Islam Malaysia Bhd, has launched the Shariah-compliant BIMB Opportunities Fund (BOF). BIMB Investment said the new fund expands its product offerings and is designed for investors seeking exposure to emerging businesses with strong growth prospects. Bank Islam group chief business officer for institutional banking Sharifah Sarah Syed Mohamed Tahir said the fund reflects its commitment to enhancing its retail investment offerings and helping investors build sustainable wealth through diversified investment solutions.

items.title: [UPDATED] Israel and Iran must 'immediately stop "shooting"': Trump
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Monday told Iran and Israel to stop fighting after the two foes attacked each other's territory for the first time since a shaky ceasefire put five weeks of war on hold. Iran fired dozens of missiles at Israel overnight and Israel responded by targeting military sites in the Islamic republic, sparking fears the escalation could usher in a new full-scale conflict after the April 8 truce. "Israel and Iran must immediately stop 'shooting.'

items.title: AmBank to buy Menara AmBank in RM331mil deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AMMB Holdings Bhd (AmBank) plans to buy Menara AmBank here from AmFIRST Real Estate Investment Trust (AmFIRST Reit) for RM331 million. Menara AmBank, located in the Golden Triangle, is a freehold commercial office building with a net lettable area of 453,419 square feet. For the financial year ended March 31, 2025, Menara AmBank generated rental income of about RM19.9 million and net property income of RM9.4 million.

items.title: Sarawak twins gain new experiences through NS training in Pahang
items.summary: PEKAN: Being selected for the National Service Training Programme (PLKN) 3.0 gave Sarawak twins Alfred Juan Ellina and Albert Noel the opportunity to travel to Peninsular Malaysia. Juan and Noel, both 18, from Bintulu, Sarawak, described their 45-day stay at the 505th Territorial Army Regiment Camp here as an eye-opener, allowing them to mingle with people of different races, hear and speak various dialects, and experience a wide range of local food. Juan said the programme also gave them the opportunity to travel to the east coast state, and they plan to return in the future.

items.title: Businesses invited to champion environmental excellence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR:The Prime Minister's Hibiscus Award committee 2026/2027 is calling on businesses to take part in one of Malaysia's longest-running platforms recognising outstanding environmental management practices. Federation of Malaysian Manufacturing president Jacob Lee Chor Kok said environmental management can no longer be viewed as a standalone or performative exercise. This includes environmental governance, compliance, operational management and carbon footprint management.

items.title: Fisheries Dept launches ‘no single-use plastic’ campaign at 56 marine parks across Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, June 8 — The Department of Fisheries Malaysia (DOF) is rolling out a “No Single-Use Plastic” campaign at 56 island marine parks nationwide in a decisive move to protect marine ecosystems from pollution and the growing threat of microplastics. Fisheries director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said the campaign, launched in conjunction with World Oceans Day 2026, calls on tourists, resort operators and tourism players to rethink their use of plastic bags when visiting marine park islands. “This threatens not just marine life, coral reefs and seagrass but also human health,” he told reporters after the World Oceans Day 2026 celebration at Pulau Redang Marine Park here today.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain in five states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds are expected to hit five states until 7pm today. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) in a statement said that the situation is expected to occur in Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Hilir Perak and Batang Padang in Perak; Cameron Highlands in Pahang; and Johor (Kluang, Mersing, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru). The same warning was also issued in Sarawak, involving Kuching (Lundu), Sarikei, Sibu (Kanowit and Selangau), Mukah (Tanjung Manis and Daro), Bintulu (Tatau), Miri (Beluru, Miri and Marudi) and Limbang (Limbang).

items.title: Mum arrested after 8-year-old girl dies, brother critically injured in 12th-floor apartment fall
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police arrested a 37-year-old Indonesian woman following the death of her eight-year-old daughter and serious injuries suffered by her 10-year-old son after both children fell from the 12th floor of an apartment building in Tampoi here yesterday. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said police received a report on the incident at 8.27am after the two children were found to have fallen from the apartment block. Acting on information police arrested the children's mother at noon on the same day to facilitate investigations.

items.title: Fire destroys 15 houses in Kota Baru
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The number of houses destroyed in a fire at Jalan Kampung Cina here today has risen from 10 to 15. Kelantan Fire and Rescue Department Zone 1 chief Khairul Ariffin Che Ramli said the department received a distress call at 12.34pm before the first fire engine from the Kota Baru Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene five minutes later. "The operation involved four fire engines, a water tanker, 38 fire personnel, as well as five volunteer firemen.

items.title: Rescued hiker Jaslinda down 10kg but in stable condition after 14-day mountain ordeal
items.summary: TAPAH, June 8 — Female climber Jaslinda Saludin has lost almost 10 kilogrammes after being missing for 14 days while climbing Gunung Batu Putih. Tapah Hospital director Dr Wan Immi Salim however said that Jaslinda, 49, was in very stable condition and was able to eat and communicate very well. Earlier, Dr Wan Immi accompanied Perak Human Resources, Health, Indian Community Affairs and National Integration Committee chairman Datuk A. Sivanesan and state Communications, Multimedia and NGO Committee chairman Mohd Azlan Helmi to visit Jaslinda at the hospital.

items.title: When one truck slows, traffic stops
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Heavy vehicles often cause congestion on steep highway gradients, reducing multi-lane roads to a single lane highway. "Once overtaking starts between slow-moving heavy vehicles, congestion waves can ripple backwards for kilometres. In the short term, Wan Agyl said several practical measures can help to ease congestion.

items.title: MCMC orders telcos to fix poor mobile coverage in Sabah’s Kampung Tinagat
items.summary: TAWAU, June 8 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has directed telecommunications service providers to take immediate measures to improve the quality of communications coverage in Kampung Tinagat here. In a statement today, MCMC said the directive followed a working visit and assessment of communications coverage and service performance conducted at three identified locations in the village. MCMC said Wireless Broadband Access Service (WBAS) tests found that two of the three locations assessed failed to meet the commission’s mandatory standards.

items.title: Anwar's Japan visit to see signing of four strategic MoUs
items.summary: TOKYO: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's official visit starting today is set to elevate Malaysia-Japan ties with the signing of four strategic memorandums of understanding (MoUs). Malaysian Ambassador to Japan Datuk Shahril Effendi Abd Ghany said the invitation reflects Tokyo's high regard for Malaysia, with Anwar receiving an official welcome at the Japanese Prime Minister's Office. Anwar's arrival at Haneda International Airport at 8.05pm Japan time (7.05pm in Malaysia) will be accompanied by Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim, and senior officials.

items.title: Malaysia secures RM92.8bil investments in Q1, Japan leads foreign inflows
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia continues to attract investor confidence despite global uncertainty, securing RM92.8 billion in approved investments in the first quarter (Q1) of 2026 with Japan emerging as the largest foreign investor. Japan topped foreign investment sources with RM21.5 billion, a sharp increase from RM1.6 billion a year earlier. Selangor led approved investments with RM33.5 billion, followed by Johor and Kuala Lumpur at RM16.9 billion each, Penang (RM6.2 billion) and Sarawak (RM4 billion).

items.title: MMA: Ensure enough staff before building PJ hospital
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) has urged the Health Ministry to ensure sufficient medical personnel are in place before work begins on the proposed public hospital in Petaling Jaya. MMA president Dr R. Thirunavukarasu said Malaysia faces a severe workforce deficit, with a shortage of nearly 11,000 specialists. He said the ministry could maximise existing private healthcare infrastructure rather than relying solely on new physical builds, noting that Petaling Jaya alone houses 12 private hospitals with more than 1,400 beds and over 450 registered private clinics.

items.title: Sabah Pakatan says no decision yet on election logo, cooperation plans
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 8 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) Sabah has yet to make any final decision on the logo it will use or the form of electoral cooperation it will pursue in the upcoming election, PH Sabah Information Chief Datuk Chan Foong Hin said today. He said no discussions or final decision had been made so far by PH Sabah on the use of any logo or electoral cooperation arrangement. He made the statement following a report by a local newspaper yesterday claiming that PH Sabah may contest under the Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) logo in the upcoming election.

items.title: Dzulkefly: Suicide cases remain alarmingly high despite slight drop in 2025
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia continues to face a serious challenge in addressing suicide, with 1,079 cases recorded last year despite a slight decline from the previous year, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said. Citing police data, Dr Dzulkefly said suicide cases had generally trended upwards over recent years before recording a modest decrease in 2025. Dzulkefly said the Health Ministry and Communications Ministry remained committed to strengthening strategic cooperation to address suicide through safe and responsible media reporting practices.

items.title: Petronas, Eni establish Searah JV to strengthen regional energy security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's national oil and gas company Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) and Italian oil company Eni have announced the formation of Searah, a 50:50 joint venture (JV) that combines selected assets in Malaysia and Indonesia. In a statement today, Petronas said the JV is a part of a partnership-led approach to strengthen portfolio value, support sustainable energy development and strengthen Malaysia and regional energy security. "By combining selected assets across Malaysia and Indonesia, Petronas and Eni have formed synergies in portfolios, technical expertise, and regional insights.

items.title: After Mindanao quake, APM deployed to monitor Sabah coast as tsunami alert lifted, says DPM Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM) has been mobilised to monitor coastal areas in Sandakan, Sabah, as a precaution following a strong earthquake in Mindanao, Philippines, this morning. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the areas under monitoring include Taman Harmoni, Kampung Forest, Sim-Sim, the Sandakan town centre and Pasir Putih. Ahmad Zahid, who also chairs the National Disaster Management Committee, said field monitoring has shown that the situation in the affected areas is safe and stable, with no unusual incidents so far.

items.title: AirAsia apologises to family, submits revised medical child restraint procedures to regulators after viral boarding dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — AirAsia has submitted enhanced procedures governing the use of medical child restraint devices on its flights after a family travelling with a daughter who has quadriplegic cerebral palsy was denied boarding from Singapore to Kuching last month. In a statement today, AirAsia X (AirAsia Group) chief safety and quality officer Captain Saravanan Subramaniam said the changes were aimed at improving support for passengers with special needs. Saravanan said passenger safety remained the airline’s priority and that the decision made at the time was in line with existing safety policies and operating procedures.

items.title: Debt collector changes plea to not guilty on sexual assault charge involving teenage boy in Shah Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 8 — A debt collector facing a charge of sexual assault involving a teenage boy changed his plea to not guilty at the Sessions Court here today. Mohamad Fadwan Abd Muaain, 42, made the plea change after the charge was read out again in the presence of Judge Rasyihah Ghazali. On May 22, Mohamad Fadwan pleaded guilty to the charge at the Sessions Court, with today’s proceedings set for the presentation of facts and mitigation before sentencing.

items.title: Terengganu firefighters rescue injured climber after six-hour trek through rain and steep terrain at Gunung Chemerong
items.summary: Terengganu Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) deputy director, Senior Fire Superintendent I, Shahrin Yusmar Mat Yusof said his department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 1.21pm yesterday before six firefighters were rushed to the location to carry out the rescue operation. He said the climbing activity was attended by 27 participants, including eight women from the Teachers and Staff Club of SMK Puncak Alam 3, Selangor, as well as four mountain guides. Shahrin Yusmar said the distance to the location from the foot of the mountain was seven kilometres and it took six hours to reach the location.

items.title: National Service 4.0 to double intake, 10,520 youths targeted as six new camps set to open nationwide
items.summary: PEKAN, June 8 — The National Service Training Department (JLKN) is targeting 10,520 participants to join the National Service Training Programme (PLKN) 4.0 at six camps nationwide next year. He told reporters after the National Service Training Parade for the 2/2026 PLKN 3.0 Series 2 at the 505 Territorial Army (AW) National Service Training Camp, here today. Meanwhile, he said a total of 1,369 PLKN 3.0 trainees successfully completed the 45-day training at three camps held simultaneously today.

items.title: FT minister seeks end to Special OSC in Kuala Lumpur planning approvals over interference concerns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Federal Territories minister said she will no longer convene Kuala Lumpur’s Special One Stop Centre (OSC) and will seek Cabinet approval to abolish the body, saying it could create room for interference or the perception of interference in development decisions. In a statement today, Hannah Yeoh said the move forms part of the government’s broader governance reform agenda aimed at strengthening integrity and transparency in Kuala Lumpur’s urban planning system. The minister said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) had already introduced measures aimed at improving transparency and accountability in planning approvals.

items.title: Music teacher in Klang pleads not guilty to 14 charges of sexual assault on six students
items.summary: KLANG, June 8 — A male teacher pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to 14 charges of committing physical sexual assault against six 15-year-old female students at a secondary school in Sabak Bernam. The accused, Idi Johan Mohd Zohdi, 45, a music teacher, entered his plea after all charges were read out before Judge Sharifah Hascindie Syed Omar. According to the charges, the man allegedly committed various acts of physical sexual assault against the six female students.

items.title: Shrimp ban sparks tension as Malaysia demands answers, Thailand threatens WTO escalation over import curbs
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, June 8 — Malaysia is awaiting Thailand’s response to a questionnaire on shrimp imports before assessing the country’s compliance with local biosecurity requirements following the ongoing trade dispute involving the commodity. Adnan said Malaysia has also strengthened its biosecurity measures on imports of sea bass from Thailand following Bangkok’s decision to impose restrictions and conduct full inspections on Malaysian sea bass exports. “Our sea bass exports are also subjected to restrictions and comprehensive inspections by Thailand.

items.title: Ex‑Army chief’s wife’s jewellery seized under MACC Act, court warns assets forfeited if no claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Sessions Court issued a third-party notice today to anyone wishing to challenge the forfeiture of jewellery seized from Salwani Anuar @ Kamaruddin, wife of former Malaysian Army chief Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan. The third-party notice was issued under Section 41(2) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009 after the court allowed an application by deputy public prosecutor Mahadi Abdul Jumaat. Salwani was also charged at the Kuala Terengganu Sessions Court on January 26 with receiving RM5,000, also proceeds from illegal activities, allegedly deposited into her account at a bank in Jertih, near Besut, on January 16, 2025.

items.title: PM Anwar heads to Japan to meet Takaichi, push new Malaysia‑Japan cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim departed for Tokyo today to lead the Malaysian delegation on the Madani Diplomacy mission to strengthen strategic cooperation and national energy security, as the world faces a global energy supply crisis. In a Facebook post, Anwar said that during the visit, he will hold a meeting with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to discuss the development of bilateral relations and to pioneer new Malaysia-Japan cooperation, particularly in the fields of economy, investment and energy security. Yesterday, the Foreign Ministry in a statement announced that Anwar’s official visit to Japan will begin today until June 10 at the invitation of the Japanese government.

items.title: Police: Three‑year‑old girl injured in Sungai Petani crash reported stable, still receiving treatment
items.summary: The victim, Aulia Sofea Ahmad Shafiq, who sustained serious injuries, received treatment at Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital (HSAH) here before being transferred to Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB) in Alor Setar today. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said she is still undergoing treatment. “The three-year-old girl is still receiving treatment.

items.title: Tumpat court fines seven over attack on driving instructor linked to alleged derogatory remarks about student
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — Seven individuals, including a woman, were each fined RM2,000 by the Magistrates’ Court in Tumpat today after pleading guilty to a charge of rioting that left a driving instructor injured last month. Utusan Malaysia reported that the accused — Azli Mamat, 48; Mohd Azaki Mamat, 42; Mohd Khadri Ghani, 52; Ahmad Faizal Juhari, 35; Muhammad Alif Hakimi Abd Hamid, 22; Muhammad Izzat Romli, 25; and Nor Hasida Mamat, 38 — entered their pleas before Magistrate Wan Ashrul Afham Mohamad Azmi. Deputy public prosecutor Mahfuzah Hamizah Mohd Ariff prosecuted the case, while lawyer Shamsul Baharin Abdullah represented all seven accused.

items.title: New MACC chief orders all officers to declare assets within one month, pledges to lead by example
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner, Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman, has required all agency personnel to submit or update their asset declarations within one month of receiving an official notification, which will be issued at a later date. He said the directive was among the first measures introduced under his leadership to strengthen the agency’s integrity and rebuild public confidence in the MACC as an independent, professional and impartial institution. He also emphasised that the MACC would not yield to pressure from any source, whether public sentiment, media scrutiny or political influence.

items.title: Nancy Shukri says new care standards will safeguard dignity, rights across Malaysia’s care services
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The National Care Standards for Older Persons and Persons with Disabilities is expected to be launched in October this year as part of ongoing efforts to strengthen Malaysia’s care economy framework. Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the standards are currently under development, following continuous discussions to establish clear benchmarks for quality care services, including expectations for providers within the care sector. “We are developing standards of care.

items.title: Telecoms boost in Perak as new tower resolves Gua Tempurung blind spot along PLUS highway
items.summary: GOPENG, June 8 — There are no stretches along the North-South Expressway (PLUS) in Perak currently experiencing complete telecommunications coverage disruptions or blind spots, said State Communications, Multimedia and NGO Committee chairman Mohd Azlan Helmi He said this was after a new telecommunications tower in the Gua Tempurung area began operations a month ago. Mohd Azlan said the Gua Tempurung area had previously been identified as one of the main locations experiencing telecommunications coverage disruptions, especially for users travelling through hilly stretches of road from the north and south. Mohd Azlan said that apart from Gua Tempurung, the state government had previously addressed telecommunications coverage issues in the Menora Tunnel area.

items.title: ‘What is wrong is wrong’: Home minister says police ready to pursue Kamunting prison probe based on Suhakam findings
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — The government is prepared to undertake further investigations into the findings and conclusions of the public inquiry panel convened by the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia over last year’s incident involving the alleged ill-treatment of a newly admitted inmate at Kamunting Prison in Taiping, Perak. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the Royal Malaysia Police is expected to carry out or complete investigations into the incident based on Suhakam’s report, which he recently presented to the Cabinet. On May 27, media reports said the Malaysian Prisons Department would fully cooperate with relevant authorities in reviewing the report and conclusions of Suhakam’s public inquiry panel into the alleged mistreatment of a new prisoner at Taiping Prison last year.

items.title: IT manager, assistant plead not guilty to 10 bribery charges linked to illegal Bitcoin mining raids in PJ
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 8 — An IT company manager and assistant pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here to 10 bribery charges for allegedly obtaining confidential information on raids against illegal Bitcoin mining. Lai Chung Lam, 40, and Lee Chee Yong, 43, pleaded not guilty before Judge Awang Kerisnada Awang Mahmud after all charges were read out to them jointly. The bribes were allegedly given in exchange for information regarding raids conducted by Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB) against premises suspected of carrying out illegal Bitcoin mining activities through electricity theft.
